symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent focal motor seizures — blank staring with rhythmic right-arm jerking (~30s) and post-ictal drowsiness
symptoms.duration: two episodes of about 30 seconds each
symptoms.character: focal motor with impaired awareness, then post-ictal lethargy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stereotyped focal motor semiology with post-ictal drowsiness in known epilepsy is a seizure presentation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: escalation of baseline Angelman movement disorder (non-epileptic)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Discrete impaired-awareness staring spells with a clear post-ictal phase are not the patient's continuous baseline jerky movements.

symptoms.symptom_name: Missed evening sodium valproate dose (sub-therapeutic anticonvulsant level)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A missed dose lowering the plasma level below therapeutic range is the classic precipitant of breakthrough seizures.
A missed dose produces a low level, excluding a toxic (excess) mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Escalation above baseline of hand-flapping, unprovoked laughter and excitable agitation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peri-ictal agitation and behavioural escalation commonly accompany a breakthrough-seizure state.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult infection or pain driving distress in a non-verbal child
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Behaviour change is non-specific in a non-verbal child and mandates screening for a treatable driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Worsening wide-based ataxic gait with repeated collisions into furniture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sub-therapeutic, peri-ictal, intermittently post-ictal state worsens the baseline ataxia.
Unsteady agitated movement in a hard-edged environment is an immediate head-injury hazard.

symptoms.symptom_name: Return to hyperactive baseline between episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Discrete episodes with inter-ictal recovery fit focal seizures rather than a sustained encephalopathic process.
A rapidly fluctuating course with full recovery between episodes is against an ongoing CNS infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no ear-directed distress, no meningism reported (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, photophobia and neck stiffness argues strongly against CNS infection.
No fever or ear-directed behaviour reduces the likelihood of otitis media as the driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs — HR 110, BP 105/65, Temp 37.1 C, RR 20, SpO2 98%, Wt 22 kg

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia is consistent with a peri-ictal/agitated state; the afebrile temperature argues against infection. Sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor infers the clinical state from carer report, not measured vitals.

symptoms.symptom_name: Microcephaly and hypopigmentation of skin and hair (baseline syndromic phenotype)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with the established syndromic diagnosis; non-contributory to the ACUTE seizure diagnosis and not the acute question.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum sodium valproate level (would be sub-therapeutic)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The sub-therapeutic mechanism is inferred from the missed-dose history rather than confirmed; does not change acute management.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: breakthrough focal seizures from a missed dose
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sub-therapeutic level confirms the mechanism — a hospital/lab investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: breakthrough focal seizures from a missed anticonvulsant dose
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Recurrent focal seizures with inter-ictal recovery on the background of a missed valproate dose is the diagnostic core and the reason for urgent in-person escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person paediatric clinical assessment (seizure review)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A child with a recurrent breakthrough-seizure cluster needs in-person examination, supervised anticonvulsant catch-up dosing, and a rescue-medication plan that cannot be delivered over telehealth.
Confirms the sub-therapeutic mechanism and guides re-dosing; performed in person, not a reason to delay escalation.
Not indicated in an afebrile child with no meningism and rapid inter-ictal recovery; over-investigation would be an error given a clear missed-dose precipitant.
